data.title: Polymarket's probability that "gold will fall below $4,700 by the end of March" has risen to 95%.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that the probability of gold falling below $4,700 by the end of March on Polymarket has rapidly risen to 95%, a 56% increase in the last 24 hours. Gold prices have indeed fallen sharply today, with current gold futures prices below $4,700 per ounce. Note: This data is based on the settlement price of the CME Group's gold (GC) futures main contract, not the spot price. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Global assets were generally under pressure, with precious metals and European stock indices experiencing significant pullbacks.
data.description: According to the latest data from Gate, gold prices fell to $4,557.45 per ounce, a daily drop of 5.42%. Silver prices fell to $67.852 per ounce, a daily drop of 9.97%. The BVIX (BTC Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 55.31, a daily increase of 5.88%. The EVIX (ETH Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 78.65, a daily increase of 3.26%. In the foreign exchange market, the USD/CNH exchange rate rose 0.08% to 6.90547. The USD/JPY exchange rate fell 0.56% to 158.787. In global stock indices, the Euro Stoxx 50 fell 1.25% to 5,578.89 points; the UK FTSE 100 fell 2.17% to 10,004.82 points; and the German DAX 40 fell 1.53% to 22,789.13 points. In commodities, WTI crude oil fell 1.94% to $97.61 per barrel, while Brent crude oil rose 1.66% to $112.74 per barrel. Gate allows users to directly trade traditional financial market products on its platform, providing a one-stop solution for precious metals, forex, global equity CFDs, major indices, and commodities, achieving deep integration of crypto assets and traditional financial assets. Gate TradFi features have been fully integrated into the Gate App and Web platform, allowing users to easily participate in global asset price trading without switching platforms, unlocking more strategies and opportunities outside the crypto market, and continuously enhancing their diversified asset allocation experience.

data.title: Qatar Energy CEO Warns of Potential Force Majeure on LNG Contracts with Italy, Belgium, and South Korea
data.description: Qatar Energy's CEO has indicated that the company may need to declare force majeure on long-term liquefied natural gas (LNG) supply contracts with Italy, Belgium, and South Korea. According to Jin10, the potential declaration could affect contracts for up to five years. This announcement comes amid ongoing challenges in the global energy market, which have impacted supply chains and contract fulfillment. The CEO's statement highlights the difficulties faced by energy providers in maintaining consistent supply amidst geopolitical and economic pressures. The potential force majeure declaration underscores the volatility in the energy sector and the need for adaptive strategies to manage unforeseen disruptions.

data.title: UAE and Egypt Discuss Regional Security Amid Rising Tensions
data.description: On March 19, UAE President Mohammed met with visiting Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi in Abu Dhabi. According to Jin10, the leaders discussed bilateral cooperation and the current situation in the Middle East. Both parties expressed concern over the severe impact of ongoing military escalations on regional and international security and stability. President el-Sisi affirmed Egypt's support for the UAE's measures to safeguard national security, territorial integrity, and the safety of its people. They emphasized the need to halt the escalation immediately and resolve regional issues through dialogue and diplomacy to prevent further tensions and crises, thereby maintaining regional security and stability.

data.title: Germany Considers Windfall Tax as Fuel Prices Surge Due to Iran Conflict
data.description: Fuel prices have surged significantly as a result of the ongoing conflict in Iran. According to Jin10, Germany is contemplating the introduction of a windfall tax to address the economic impact of rising fuel costs. The conflict has disrupted oil supplies, leading to increased prices globally. Germany's potential tax measure aims to mitigate the financial burden on consumers and stabilize the market. The situation remains tense, with geopolitical factors continuing to influence the energy sector.

data.title: Pro-Kremlin Activist Criticizes Putin Amid Ukraine Conflict
data.description:
Ilya Remeslo, a prominent pro-Kremlin activist, has publicly criticized Russian President Vladimir Putin, marking a notable shift in sentiment regarding the ongoing conflict in Ukraine. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Remeslo's unexpected stance, which reflects growing dissatisfaction with the war among some Kremlin supporters.

Remeslo, who has been known for his attacks on Russian opposition leader Alexey Navalny, expressed his discontent with Putin's handling of the situation. This criticism comes as the war in Ukraine continues to strain Russia's resources and international relations.

The activist's comments underscore a broader fatigue with the prolonged conflict, which has seen significant casualties and economic repercussions. As the war drags on, voices of dissent within Russia are becoming more pronounced, challenging the government's narrative and approach.

This development adds to the complexities faced by the Kremlin as it navigates both domestic and international pressures related to the Ukraine war. The criticism from a previously staunch supporter like Remeslo may signal a shift in public opinion, potentially influencing future political dynamics in Russia.

data.title: S&P 500 Stocks Show Extreme Oversold Levels
data.description: As of today, 48.2% of S&P 500 stocks are considered oversold, while only 10.2% are overbought. Bespoke Investment Group posted on X that these figures represent the most extreme levels since the tariff-related market fluctuations of last spring. This trend indicates a significant shift in market dynamics, reflecting investor sentiment and market volatility. The current oversold condition suggests potential opportunities for investors, although it also highlights underlying market concerns. The disparity between oversold and overbought stocks underscores the ongoing uncertainty in the financial markets.

data.title: Crypto Hacks Persist with Concentrated Losses in Major Exploits
data.description:
A recent security report by Immunefi reveals that while the frequency of crypto hacks remains steady, the financial losses are increasingly concentrated in a few large-scale exploits. According to Cointelegraph, the report analyzed 425 publicly known incidents from 2021 to 2025, estimating an average hack loss of approximately $25 million. In 2024 and 2025 alone, 191 hacks resulted in $4.67 billion in losses, with just five incidents accounting for 62% of the total.

Centralized exchange breaches, though fewer in number, were responsible for the majority of these losses. Twenty exchange hacks contributed to around $2.55 billion, or about 55% of the total, highlighting the vulnerability of large pools of user funds concentrated in fewer points of failure. The report also noted that token markets are reacting more severely to breaches. Among 82 hacked tokens tracked, prices fell a median of 61% within six months, with 83.9% remaining below their hack-day price during that period. Immunefi CEO Mitchell Amador explained that the market's expectations have shifted, viewing breaches as indicators of deeper issues in engineering, governance, and operational resilience.

Amador emphasized that the impact of exploits extends beyond the initial financial loss, affecting token prices, treasury capacity, leadership, development time, and user trust. The report also highlighted the interconnected nature of DeFi systems, where a single incident can have cascading effects across lending, collateral, and liquidity networks. An example is the collapse of Elixir’s deUSD stablecoin in November 2025, which was linked to a $93 million loss by Stream Finance, leading to a significant devaluation of deUSD.

Despite a decrease in crypto-related hack losses to $26.5 million in February, the lowest monthly total in nearly a year, several security incidents have emerged in March. Google researchers identified a new exploit kit, Coruna, targeting Apple iPhone users to steal cryptocurrency wallet seed phrases. Additionally, the Bitcoin-based DeFi platform Solv Protocol reported a $2.7 million exploit affecting fewer than 10 users, with the project offering a bounty for the return of funds. Meanwhile, the domain of Bonk.fun was hijacked, leading to a wallet-draining scheme, and NFT lending platform Gondi disabled a faulty smart contract after an exploit resulted in the theft of approximately $230,000 worth of NFTs. The project is compensating affected users while investigating the vulnerability.

data.title: Solana's official account posted a message hinting that Backpack will launch with Solana.
data.description: Solana's official account posted a message hinting that Backpack will be launching Solana.

data.title: Zenith Completes First Atomic Transactions Between Networks
data.description: Zenith has announced the successful completion of its first atomic transactions between the Canton Network and the Zenith EVM. According to NS3.AI, this achievement follows the processing of over 100,000 EVM transactions during testing. Zenith reported that transaction latency typically ranged from 400 milliseconds to 1.5 seconds. This milestone aims to integrate Ethereum-style smart contracts with Canton infrastructure, which handles over $9 trillion in monthly volume.

data.title: Cloudflare shares rose more than 6% as Coinbase competed to issue its own stablecoin, NET dollar.
data.description: Cloudflare (NYSE: NET) shares rose more than 6% on Wednesday, closing at $225.48. According to sources familiar with the matter, Coinbase (NASDAQ: COIN) and crypto infrastructure provider Zerohash are vying to become the issuer of Cloudflare's upcoming stablecoin, NET Dollar, which is scheduled to launch later this year. NET Dollar is a dollar-pegged token designed to drive seamless transactions in a "proxy network"—a next-generation internet where autonomous AI agents independently browse, negotiate, and settle payments. Cloudflare plans to embed stablecoin functionality directly into its network infrastructure to enable efficient micropayments in AI-driven activities, including content access, data processing, and automated services. This project builds on Cloudflare's strategic partnership with Coinbase last year, which jointly established the x402 Foundation to promote an open standard that reinstates the HTTP 402 "Payment Required" status code, enabling websites and developers to natively request and receive instant digital payments in browsers and applications. Zerohash has regulatory experience and technical capabilities in compliant stablecoin issuance and liquidity services.

data.title: South Africa's Automotive Industry Confronts Modern Challenges
data.description:
South Africa's automotive industry, with a history spanning over a century, is currently grappling with a range of contemporary issues. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the sector is experiencing significant challenges that threaten its longstanding stability and growth.

The industry, which has been a cornerstone of South Africa's economy, is now facing pressures from global supply chain disruptions, technological advancements, and shifting consumer preferences. These factors are compelling manufacturers to adapt rapidly to maintain their competitive edge.

Additionally, the transition towards electric vehicles and sustainable practices is posing both opportunities and hurdles for the industry. Companies are investing in new technologies to align with global trends, but the pace of change is testing their resilience and adaptability.

The South African government and industry stakeholders are working together to address these challenges, aiming to ensure the sector's sustainability and continued contribution to the national economy. However, the path forward requires strategic planning and investment to navigate the evolving landscape effectively.

data.title: UN Secretary-General Urges End to Conflict, Calls on Iran to Halt Attacks
data.description: United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres has called on the United States and Israel to bring an end to the ongoing conflict. According to Jin10, Guterres emphasized the urgency of resolving the situation peacefully. Additionally, he urged Iran to cease its attacks on neighboring countries, highlighting the need for regional stability and cooperation. The Secretary-General's statements come amid heightened tensions and underscore the importance of diplomatic efforts to address the crisis.

data.title: Rare-Earth Startup Vulcan Gains Support Amid Political Concerns
data.description: A rare-earth startup, Vulcan, is receiving backing from both U.S. taxpayers and U.S. President Donald Trump's son. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this support has sparked discomfort among some Republicans who are wary of state-sponsored capitalism. The involvement of public funds and political figures in the venture has raised questions about the role of government in private enterprise. Vulcan's focus on rare-earth elements is seen as strategically important, given their critical use in various technologies. However, the political implications of such investments continue to stir debate.

data.title: Arc Secures Investment from Leading Venture Firms
data.description: Arc has successfully secured investment from prominent venture capital firms, including a16z and Lowercarbon Capital, in its latest fundraising round. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the involvement of these influential investors in supporting Arc's growth and development. The funding round underscores the confidence of major venture firms in Arc's potential and future prospects.

data.title: La Niña Phenomenon Continues, U.S. Climate Prediction Center Reports
data.description: The U.S. Climate Prediction Center has reported that the La Niña phenomenon is still ongoing, based on current oceanic and atmospheric observations. According to Jin10, this climatic condition, characterized by cooler-than-average sea surface temperatures in the central and eastern Pacific Ocean, can have significant impacts on global weather patterns. La Niña events are known to influence weather conditions, potentially leading to increased rainfall in some regions and droughts in others. The persistence of La Niña could affect agricultural productivity, water resources, and disaster preparedness in various parts of the world. Monitoring and understanding these patterns are crucial for mitigating potential adverse effects.

data.title: Opera is seeking 160 million CELO tokens to become a key stakeholder in the Celo network.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Opera has submitted a proposal to the Celo governance forum to replace its existing cash payment protocol with Celo with a three-year allocation of 160 million CELO tokens. This allocation represents approximately 27% of CELO's current circulating supply of about 600 million tokens and 16% of its maximum supply of 1 billion tokens. CELO is currently trading at $0.07. The transaction would involve a one-time transfer of 160 million CELO from the never-released Treasury to a Safe account controlled by Opera. To maintain governance balance, the voting power of these tokens will be limited to 10% of the total staked CELO, except in protocol emergencies. Opera stated that the proposal reflects the important role of its self-custodied crypto wallet, MiniPay, on the Celo network. MiniPay runs on Celo, an Ethereum Layer 2 network, and has over 14 million registered users and over 420 million transactions in more than 66 countries since its launch in 2023. Opera EVP Jorgen Arnesen stated that Opera aims to become a supportive key stakeholder in the Celo ecosystem. Furthermore, over 50 million Opera browser users who have earned rewards in recent months can redeem their rewards in USDT via MiniPay. Opera's stock price is currently $14.60.

data.title: UK Competition Watchdog Investigates Adobe Over Subscription Fees
data.description: Adobe is under investigation by the UK's competition authority due to concerns about its early cancellation fees for certain products potentially violating consumer protection laws. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the scrutiny Adobe faces regarding its subscription practices. The investigation aims to determine whether these fees are unfair to consumers and if they comply with existing regulations. Adobe has yet to comment on the ongoing inquiry.

data.title: U.S. Climate Prediction Center Forecasts Transition to Neutral ENSO State
data.description: The U.S. Climate Prediction Center anticipates a shift from La Niña to a neutral El Niño-Southern Oscillation (ENSO) state over the next month. According to Jin10, the neutral ENSO condition is expected to prevail from May to July 2026. Furthermore, the emergence of El Niño is highly likely between June and August 2026, with projections indicating its persistence at least until the end of 2026.

data.title: Spot silver fell more than 10%, approaching the $67 mark.
data.description: According to Gate data, spot silver fell by more than 10%, approaching the $67 mark.
